text: Planning a trip is always exciting, but it can also be a little stressful. During my last holiday, I spent hours researching flights and hotels. When I finally arrived in a new country, everything felt different. The local food was fantastic, especially the spicy street dishes I had never tried before. However, language barriers made simple tasks difficult. Ordering a meal or asking for directions often required hand gestures and smiles. Despite this, those moments became fun memories. I learned that travel isn't just about seeing places—it's about tasting, listening, and connecting with new cultures. Now, I always pack a phrasebook and an open mind.
